name: ExtensionSpecimenDefinition_Subject
title: R5: Type of subject for specimen collection (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `SpecimenDefinition.subject[x]` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`SpecimenDefinition.subject[x]` 0..1 `CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `SpecimenDefinition.subject[x]` 0..1 `CodeableConcept, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `SpecimenDefinition.subject[x]` has a context of SpecimenDefinition based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SpecimenDefinition`.
Element `SpecimenDefinition.subject[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Type of subject for specimen collection (new)
element.comment:
Element `SpecimenDefinition.subject[x]` has a context of SpecimenDefinition based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SpecimenDefinition`.
Element `SpecimenDefinition.subject[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Examples: person, animal, device, water ….

element.short: Type of subject for specimen collection
element.definition: A code or group definition that describes the intended subject  from which this kind of specimen is to be collected.
